name: Salim ibn Amr
title: Loyal of Bani Madina
shortDescription: A sincere Shia from Kufa who reached Karbala during the difficult days before Ashura and fought valiantly on the day of Ashura.
fullStory:
Salim ibn Amr was a sincere and brave Shia from Kufa who lived in the Bani Madina clan of the Kalb tribe. He was among those who, upon hearing the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) movement towards Iraq, immediately decided to help him.

Under conditions where Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad had closed all exit routes from Kufa, Salim bravely passed through the guards and roadblocks and reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan. His presence in Karbala demonstrated an iron will and firm faith.

On the day of Ashura, Salim ibn Amr stood in the front line defending the sanctuary of Wilayah. A skilled warrior, as the enemy attacks began, he drew his sword with all his might and confronted Umar Sa'd's soldiers.

He recited war poetry in the battlefield, showcasing the bravery and righteousness of his path to the enemies. In a heroic battle, after killing a number of enemies, Salim attained martyrdom in the field of Karbala due to severe injuries.

The name of this loyal companion of the Master of Martyrs (AS) is mentioned in the Ziyarat of Nahiya al-Muqaddasa, which indicates the greatness of his status before the Ahl al-Bayt (AS).
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Salim ibn Amr.
